pages_preview.text_preview: Dear participants, welcome to our oline Replication Study, aiming to replicate the study "Why didn´t you tag me?!: Social exclusion from Instagram posts hurts, especially those with a high need to belong" original study by Christiane M. Büttner and Selma C. Rudert (2022) Why do we collect and process your data The data will be used exclusively for scientific purposes in the context of the above-mentioned study. The data will be collected anonymously. You do not need to provide any personal data (name, date of birth, etc.) in the questionnaire, so that no conclusions can be drawn about your person. How to contact us We are represented by MSH Medical School Hamburg - Geschäftsführerin: University of Applied Science and Ilona Renken-Olthoff Medical University Am Kaiserkai 1 20457 Hamburg info@medicalschool-hamburg.de 040 36122640 How long will the personal data be processed 10 year(s) What personal data are collected and processed None What special categories of personal data are collected and processed None Legal basis for the processing
Consent of the affected persons When you continue this Study, you agree to these terms. If you chose not to, please exit this study or close the tab.

Dear participants, Thank you for your interest in our replication study aiming to replicate the study "Why didn't you tag me?!: Social exclusion from Instagram posts hurts, especially those with a high need to belong" original study by Christiane M. Büttner and Selma C. Rudert (2022). Please read this information carefully before deciding whether or not to participate in the study. Who is conducting the study? The study is being conducted by master's students in the clinical psychology program with a focus on psychology and psychotherapy (1-2nd semesters) as part of a research internship under the supervision of Thomas Langkamp. How does the study work? Once you have read this study information and you have decided to participate, we will ask you to complete several questionnaires that capture different aspects and facets of who you are. The processing of the questionnaires takes about 10-20 minutes. The online questionnaire is aimed at people with active social media use. Subject hours for members of the Medical School Hamburg Upon completion of the questionnaire, you will be credited with 0.5 subject hours if you opened the link through your SONA account. What are the possible risks of participating in the study?

There are no anticipated risks associated with study participation. What is the aim of the study? The aim of the study is to find out how strongly people react to exclusion on social media and whether there are differences between specific groups, for example, whether people who show active usage behavior are more affected by exclusion. Are voluntariness and anonymity guaranteed? Participation in the study is voluntary. You may terminate your participation in this study at any time during the survey without giving reasons and without incurring any disadvantages. Furthermore, you consent to the results of the study being summarized in groups and published scientifically without reference to specific individuals. Is data protection secured? The online survey is conducted with the software "Unipark" from the company TIVIAN, which uses the so-called "SSL encryption" for the secure transmission of data on the Internet. Your IP address is stored in shortened form in the online questionnaire, so that no identification of your person is possible. Study staff will secure the collected data and store it on password-protected computers at MSH Medical School Hamburg. Only study staff will have access to this data. When transferring data within the study team, only password-protected cloud systems of MSH Medical School Hamburg will be used. If you have any further questions regarding data protection, please contact the data protection officer of MSH Medical School Hamburg Can I subsequently request deletion of my data? Your data is anonymized. This means that it is no longer possible for anyone to associate the collected data with your name. Therefore, it is not possible for your data to be deleted afterwards.

What is the retention period for the anonymized data? The retention period for the anonymized data is at least 10 years after data evaluation, or at least 10 years after a publication on this study appears. Consent form for the study "Determinants to State-Emotion Regulation" . 1. I have been informed in writing about the study and the trial procedure. I have read and understood all information in full. I have received a copy of the participation information about the study and a copy of the consent form (download link, see above). 2. I know that participation in the study is voluntary and that I can withdraw my consent to participate at any time without incurring any disadvantages. I agree to the described collection and processing of my personal data (age, gender) as well as to my information on the questions posed. The collection and evaluation of this data will be anonymous. A subsequent deletion of my own data is not possible. 3. I agree that my anonymized data can be used for research purposes and will be stored for at least 10 years. Furthermore, I agree that anonymized data, which do not reveal my identity, can be made publicly available to other scientists. 4. I had enough time to make a decision and I am willing to participate in the study "Determinants to State Emotion Regulation". If I have any questions or other concerns, I can contact: sandra.miethe@medicalschool-hamburg. If you decide to continue with this study, you agree with these terms. If you don´t agree, please exit this study and close the window. 22 BBaassiiss ddaattaa We need the following information for the statistics. A conclusion on your person is not possible and your anonymity is preserved. What is your gender? male female I cannot / do not want to assign myself
